inputs.name: pMessage
inputs.value: Formula that subtracts the time not worked entry for the scheduled shift from a reported time entry's calculated duration if the entries match shift.

metadata.description: Add node for Summary case branch
metadata.name: Add (Summary Case)

metadata.description: Add node for Query case branch
metadata.name: Add (Query Case)

dataDisplay.customNotes:
Display the response as a record. Show Message, Template, Session_Id, and Formula Text.
Formula Text should preserve line breaks and use a monospace/preformatted style if available.

inputs.name: prompt

metadata.name: jobStatusCheck

tools.description: Generate Fast Formula from natural language. Blocking - waits for LLM completion. Returns formula code in text field.
tools.name: generateFormulaSync

parameterDefinitions.description: Natural language requirement describing what the formula should do. Example: Generate a time entry rule formula that validates minimum hours per period.
parameterDefinitions.name: pMessage

parameterDefinitions.description: Business key from FF_FORMULA_TEMPLATES.TEMPLATE_CODE. Identifies the reference formula template. Always required. Example: ORA_HCM_FF_TER_MIN_HOURS_001
parameterDefinitions.name: pTemplate_code

parameterDefinitions.description: LLM model selector. Values: GPT5MINI (default, GPT-5 Mini) or GPT41MINI (GPT-4.1 Mini). Optional.
parameterDefinitions.name: pLlm

parameterDefinitions.description: Current formula code in the editor. When provided, the LLM modifies this code instead of generating from scratch. Optional.
parameterDefinitions.name: pEditor_code

parameterDefinitions.description: Session ID (Agent Studio conversationId) for multi-turn conversation. Pass back the value from a previous response to continue refining. Optional.
parameterDefinitions.name: pSession_id

query.message: Generate a time entry rule formula that validates minimum hours

sampleQueries.description: Generate a time entry rule formula with minimum hours validation using GPT-5 Mini model.
sampleQueries.stringValue: {"message":"Generate a time entry rule formula that validates minimum hours","template_code":"ORA_HCM_FF_TER_MIN_HOURS_001","llm":"GPT5MINI"}

tools.description: Submit formula generation job. Returns jobId immediately. Poll status via pollJobStatus.
tools.name: generateFormulaAsync

parameterDefinitions.description: Natural language requirement describing what the formula should do. Example: Generate a time entry rule formula that validates minimum hours per period.
parameterDefinitions.name: pMessage

parameterDefinitions.description: Business key from FF_FORMULA_TEMPLATES.TEMPLATE_CODE. Identifies the reference formula template. Always required. Example: ORA_HCM_FF_TER_MIN_HOURS_001
parameterDefinitions.name: pTemplate_code

parameterDefinitions.description: LLM model selector. Values: GPT5MINI (default, GPT-5 Mini) or GPT41MINI (GPT-4.1 Mini). Optional.
parameterDefinitions.name: pLlm

parameterDefinitions.description: Current formula code in the editor. When provided, the LLM modifies this code instead of generating from scratch. Optional.
parameterDefinitions.name: pEditor_code

parameterDefinitions.description: Session ID (Agent Studio conversationId) for multi-turn conversation. Pass back the value from a previous response to continue refining. Optional.
parameterDefinitions.name: pSession_id

sampleQueries.description: Generate a time entry rule formula with minimum hours validation using GPT-5 Mini model.

tools.description: Poll async job status by jobId. Returns status (RUNNING/COMPLETE/ERROR) and formula code when complete.
tools.name: pollJobStatus

parameterDefinitions.description: Job ID returned by the async Generate Formula endpoint (POST /chat). Used to poll execution status until COMPLETE or ERROR.
parameterDefinitions.name: pJob_Id

parameterDefinitions.description: Optional wait in seconds before polling status. Use 10 to slow Agent Studio while-loop polling. Values above 30 are capped by the service.
parameterDefinitions.name: pWait_Seconds

tools.description: List available formula templates filtered by formula type. Each template has reference code and additional rules.
tools.name: listTemplates

tools.description: List all formula types with display names.
tools.name: listFormulaTypes

tools.description: Validate Fast Formula syntax with 3-layer checks (syntax, semantic, rules).
tools.name: validateFormula

parameterDefinitions.description: Fast Formula source code to validate. The validator runs 3-layer checks: syntax parsing, semantic analysis (undeclared variables, unassigned outputs), and business rules (missing RETURN).
parameterDefinitions.name: pCode

query.code:
DEFAULT FOR hours IS 0
INPUTS ARE hours
l_pay = hours * 25
RETURN l_pay

sampleQueries.description: Validate a simple hourly pay formula with DEFAULT, INPUTS ARE, and RETURN.
sampleQueries.stringValue:
{"code":"DEFAULT FOR hours IS 0
INPUTS ARE hours
l_pay = hours * 25
RETURN l_pay"}

tools.description: Health check endpoint.
tools.name: healthCheck

boFunctionNodes.Name: Fast Formula Generator Business Object
boFunctionNodes.Description: AI-powered Fast Formula code generator for Oracle HCM. Accepts natural language requirements and returns syntactically valid Fast Formula source code. Supports formula types including Payroll, Time and Labor, and Absence Management. Provides sync, async, and streaming endpoints with multi-turn conversation support via Agent Studio.

boFunctionNodes.Name: Fast Formula Generator Business Object
boFunctionNodes.Description: AI-powered Fast Formula code generator for Oracle HCM. Accepts natural language requirements and returns syntactically valid Fast Formula source code. Supports formula types including Payroll, Time and Labor, and Absence Management. Provides sync, async, and streaming endpoints with multi-turn conversation support via Agent Studio.

Name: Payroll FF Testing1
FollowUpPrompt:
Based on the following conversion: $param.system_context.chat_history generate $param.system_context.number_of_follow_up_questions
follow up questions only while strictly adhering to the JSON Schema format mentioned below. Remove the ```json markdown from the output.
Here is the JSON Schema format the output should adhere to:
[{"question": "<put first question generated here>"}, {"question": "<put second question generated here>"}, {"question": "<put third question generated here>"}
